question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNftAllowances(NftAllowanceRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the Collection<NftAllowance> returned by getNftAllowances() to be partially populated (some rows fetched before partition) and partially empty, causing the caller to make authorization decisions based on an incomplete allowance set without any indication that the data is incomplete?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNftAllowances(NftAllowanceRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the EntityIdNumParameter path in EntityServiceImpl.lookup() to return an EntityId without any DB validation (since it uses Optional.of(p.id()) directly), allowing the attacker to query allowances for a non-existent account ID that happens to match a valid account on a partitioned replica?
